more.title: SAPS warns public after Centurion blue light arrests
more.description: The South African Police Service (SAPS) has issued a stern warning to the public after the arrest of two men in Centurion for the illegal fitting and possession of blue lights on a private vehicle. more.description: A migrant who came from Hermanus described the conditions as unlivable.

more.title: Quentin Faltain’s journey from job losses to building a proudly South African business
more.description: After three retrenchments pushed him to rethink his future, Quentin Faltain built CQC Business Solutions into an integrated workplace solutions provider focused on innovation, empowerment and sustainability.

more.title: Fuel relief arrives, but South Africans remain trapped between job losses, food costs and rising bills
more.description: A drop in fuel prices offers welcome relief, but South African households remain under pressure as unemployment rises, food costs stay elevated and municipal bills increase.

more.title: South Africa Immigration Protests: R600 Million Security Operation and the Call for an ‘Ubuntu’ Approach
more.description: DURBAN, South Africa — The recent wave of South Africa immigration protests has prompted a massive R600 million security operation and sparked urgent calls for a compassionate, Ubuntu-led policy approach to the country’s complex border challenges. more.title: South African Scientists Shine at 75th Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting in Germany
more.description: South Africa's next generation of scientists is making its mark on the global stage at the 75th Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting in Lindau, Germany, where some of the world's brightest early-career researchers are engaging directly with Nobel Prize winners and leading scientific thinkers. more.title: EXCLUSIVE | Letsoaka urges Safa to retain Broos in advisory role for smooth Bafana Bafana transition
more.description: Former Bafana assistant Serame Letsoaka advises SAFA to retain Hugo Broos temporarily, ensuring a smooth transition and continuity for the national team’s upcoming qualifiers.

more.title: UK Promoter Y2K Entertainment Cuts All Ties With South African Music Industry Over Migrant Crisis Backlash
more.description: South Africa’s music industry has taken a serious international hit. UK-based Y2K Entertainment Promotions, a long-running promoter of Southern African music, announced on 2 July that it is withdrawing all support for South African artists. The Y2K Entertainment boycott is being framed by the company as a direct response to the anti-illegal immigration protests that [...] The post UK Promoter Y2K Entertainment Cuts All Ties With South African Music Industry Over Migrant Crisis Backlash appeared first on SurgeZirc SA .

more.title: Watch: South Africans in stitches as locals learn to ride Sixty60
more.description: The videos are out as the locals learn to ride Sixty60. The reactions are louder. And South Africa is equal parts encouraging and crying laughing, reports Cape {town} Etc. more.title: Gayton McKenzie gives away 100 free Springboks vs England tickets
more.description: Gayton McKenzie has launched a last-minute ticket giveaway ahead of Saturday's Test between the Springboks and England at Ellis Park.

more.title: Springboks face ticket sales challenges ahead of England Test match
more.description: SA Rugby attributes slow ticket sales for the Springboks' opening Test against England to external factors, including the war in Iran, while captain Siya Kolisi emphasises the team's connection with fans.

more.title: Cash at home: why many South Africans still avoid formal savings
more.description: Nearly half of South Africans continue to save cash at home despite wider access to banking services, exposing themselves to theft, fire and lost interest, according to FinMark Trust and World Bank data. Experts say trust, accessibility and financial habits remain major barriers to formal saving.

more.title: Public Ambulance Shortage in South Africa Sparks Parliamentary Outcry
more.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa — A growing public ambulance shortage in South Africa has become a focal point of concern in Parliament, with opposition parties warning that vulnerable citizens are being left behind.
